event1.text: I found a route under the relay spine.

event3.text: It leads to the personnel archive. Psych files. Assignment indexes. The kind of place management used when it wanted people to look calm on paper.

event5.text: I don't want to go in there.

event7.text: Which probably means we have to.

backgroundEvent8.text: Maya, if the archive still has power, assume it still has teeth.

event3.text: Fast and stupid. My favorite combination when oxygen is low.

event5.text: I'm crossing the relay spine. Floor looks warped, and the support ribs are sweating condensation.

event7.text: I need to move with both hands free. If the channel goes quiet, I am not gone. I'm just busy not dying.

event3.text: Slower. Tighter. Less likely to kill me immediately. Very luxurious.

event5.text: I'm going in on my side. Relay pack first, then me. I already hate this order.

event7.text: I'll report when I reach the archive hatch.

event3.text: It wants either a manual open or a safety scan.

event5.text: The casing is warm. Too warm for an archive hatch that should be asleep.

event7.text: I can force it now, or we spend a minute making sure it isn't wired to bite.

event8.text: I'm counting that as a very ECHO-shaped win.

backgroundEvent9.text: OPEN IS A KIND OF CONSENT.

event3.text: I'm keeping one hand off the panel until the loop answers.

event5.text: Hale, if this thing tries to erase my fingerprints, I'm blaming you retroactively.

backgroundEvent6.text: Careful is not cowardly. I know that ruins Maya's entire brand, but there it is.

event3.text: Rows of archive cabinets. Physical drives. Paper labels. Actual paper, Operator.

event5.text: Management couldn't keep life support stable, but they had time to label trauma alphabetically.

event7.text: I see entries for Hale, me... and an empty slot tagged VOSS.

event9.text: The VOSS drawer has no handle. Just a clean rectangle where one used to be.

event0.title: PSYCHOLOGICAL EVALUATION - ETHAN HALE
event0.content:
Subject: Ethan Hale
Status: Stable
Risk Level: Low
Recommendation: Active Duty Approved
Evaluator Note: Subject demonstrates strong command resilience under isolation pressure.
Created: 12 April
Linked Incident: Alpha
event0.caption: Recovered psychological evaluation record. Timestamp appears inconsistent.

event4.text: That's generous. I once threatened a printer with decompression.

event6.text: It had been jamming for three weeks, so history may yet vindicate me.

event8.text: But the date is wrong. April 12 is after Alpha.

event10.text: That means this is not a wellness assessment. It's a cleanup document.

event0.title: PSYCHOLOGICAL EVALUATION - MAYA CHEN
event0.content:
Subject: Maya Chen
Status: Stable
Risk Level: Low
Recommendation: Active Duty Approved
Evaluator Note: Subject shows high pattern-recognition ability and repeated resistance to memory suppression protocols.
Created: 12 April
Linked Incident: Alpha
event0.caption: Recovered psychological evaluation record. Note contains memory suppression reference.

event4.text: Nobody told us there were memory protocols.

event6.text: That means they tried. And someone wrote it down like a treatment plan.

event11.text: No. I don't get a second. Keep me moving.

event1.text: We need the other file before this becomes a story we invent to survive it.

event3.text: I am very good at inventing stories.

event5.text: That is not a compliment right now.

event3.text: Angry, oxygen-starved, and very close to screaming, but here.

event5.text: If I start spiraling, talk to me like I'm fixing something.

event0.title: PSYCH EVALUATION CONFLICT SUMMARY
event0.content:
HALE: Marked stable after Incident Alpha.
MAYA: Repeated resistance to memory suppression protocols.
VOSS: Archive slot exists, record body removed.
INDEX: Archive created after Incident Alpha date.

Conflict: Evaluations appear to be post-incident control records, not pre-incident wellness assessments.
event0.caption: Combined evaluation conflict summary generated from recovered records.
event0.message: [EVALUATION CONFLICT SUMMARY ARCHIVED TO DATA BANK]

event2.text: They didn't want to know if we were okay.

event4.text: They wanted the file to say we were.

event10.text: For once, I hate how much sense the ghost makes.

event1.text: Good. Then help me uncover the truth.

event3.text: And if Hale gets calm again, remember this moment.

event5.text: Calm is his emergency voice.

event3.text: Some truths are dangerous because once you know them, something knows you know.

event5.text: That sounds paranoid.

event7.text: I miss when paranoid was a personality flaw and not a survival method.

backgroundEvent8.text: You hear him? That's the voice. Gentle. Terrified. Trying to make fear sound like wisdom.

event1.text: There's another terminal behind the archive racks.

event3.text: Personnel Archive Cross-Check.

event5.text: I can run it remotely, or I can physically pull the original assignment index drive.

event7.text: Remote is cleaner. Drive is riskier, but harder for the archive to lie about.

event9.text: My vote is the drive. My lungs disagree. Very democratic in here.

event3.text: The terminal keeps asking me to confirm I want the original index.

event5.text: Very comforting. Love when a machine develops hesitation.

backgroundEvent6.text: If it asks twice, read the second prompt. Old systems hide knives in confirmation screens.

event3.text: Rack is live. I can feel it humming through my gloves.

event5.text: If I go quiet, it means I'm using both hands and trying not to bleed on the evidence.

event0.title: GHOST CREW COUNT REPORT
event0.content:
OFFICIAL MANIFEST COUNT: 6
RECOVERED MANIFEST LAYER COUNT: 7
ORIGINAL ASSIGNMENT INDEX COUNT: 8

Discrepancy: one person removed from official manifest. One additional person removed from recovered manifest layer.
Visual archive unavailable. Count derived from personnel hashes, suit registry keys and life support allocation records.
event0.caption: Personnel count reconstructed without image records.
event0.message: [GHOST CREW COUNT REPORT ARCHIVED TO DATA BANK]

event1.text: If Noah was seven, then someone else was eight.

event3.text: And I don't like how close that thought feels to a memory.

event5.text: COUNT AGAIN.

backgroundEvent8.text: Operator... there is a sealed subfolder under the eighth slot.

seal_eighth_slot.text: SEAL IT AND GET MAYA OUT.

event3.text: No clever comment. I don't have one.
event3.title: EIGHTH SLOT FRAGMENT
event3.content:
Assignment Index Slot: 08
Name Field: CORRUPTED
Suit Registry Echo: NV-08 adjacent
Pod Link: External emergency pod
Access Note: DO NOT RESTORE WHILE SUBJECT IS BREATHING
event3.caption: Fragment recovered from the sealed eighth assignment slot. Opening this record exposed the pod link.
event3.message: [EIGHTH SLOT FRAGMENT ARCHIVED TO DATA BANK]

event3.text: I hate this. Not because you were wrong.

event5.text: Because part of me is relieved you stopped me.

event7.text: And I don't know what that says about me.

event1.text: The archive lights just came on behind me.

event3.text: Not the hallway. Behind me.

event5.text: I am leaving now. No vote. No debate. Very democratic of me.

event1.text: I'm getting out before I change my mind.

event4.text: I am so tired of things remembering for us.

event6.text: Then we go to the pod.

event10.text: You always say that right before everything gets worse.

event2.text: I'm at the archive access hatch.

event4.text: The floor flexed under me halfway across. Not a little. Enough that my stomach moved before I did.

event6.text: Something scraped behind the wall. I kept moving.

event8.text: If it wanted a conversation, it can file a request.

event1.text: I'm at the archive access hatch.

event3.text: The crawlspace was quiet. Not comforting quiet. Listening quiet.

event5.text: There were boot prints in the dust. Not mine.

event7.text: Someone came through after the power failed. They were dragging something.

event9.text: So we gained signal stability and a new reason to sleep badly later.

event3.text: No active hooks. Or one old enough to pretend it's harmless.

event5.text: The panel asked for confirmation twice. Hale was right about the second prompt.

event3.text: It asked twice. Hale was right again, which is becoming personally inconvenient.

event5.text: The numbers are wrong in layers. Not one lie. Several lies stacked politely on top of each other.

event3.text: The archive did not like that.

event5.text: I cut my hand on the rack lip. Not badly. Enough to be annoying.

event7.text: If I die from archive shelving, lie about it.

event3.text: It linked to a pod.

event5.text: Not a record. Not a memorial. A pod.

event9.text: Don't ask me to make that make sense.

event11.text: I am trying very hard not to remember something that wants to be remembered.

event3.text: The slot is sealed. The archive accepted it, which somehow makes me trust it less.

event5.text: I can still feel the unopened folder sitting behind my eyes.

event7.text: I'm getting out before I change my mind.

event4.text: Something moved in the racks while I was leaving. I did not check what.

event6.text: Hale told me to keep my eyes forward.

event8.text: I did. I hated obeying him almost as much as I hated needing to.

event10.text: Shaking. Breathing. Angry at both of those facts.

event3.text: I kept thinking about the name we didn't see.

event5.text: Hale said sometimes not opening a door is the only reason you're alive to hate yourself for it.

event11.text: So the archive wasn't the end of this. Of course it wasn't.
